agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to review 2026-27 operating budget and capital plan
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council will review a balanced draft operating budget for FY 2026-27 and a five-year capital improvement plan (2026-2031). Key proposed priorities include wastewater system reliability (Veolia contract expires May 2027), parks renovations at Boorman, Wendell, and Shields-Reid parks, city-wide systems modernization, and ALS first responder services. The final budget adoption is scheduled for June 23, 2026. The CIP includes $1.25M for an e-bike lending library, $3.87M for the Richmond Wellness Trail Phase II, and $4.28M for the SF Bay Trail at Point Molate, among other projects.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action required now. Watch for the final budget adoption meeting on June 23, 2026 — it could affect city fees, permits, or services your business relies on.

key_dates.label: Final budget adoption — Richmond City Council

RECEIVE an overview of the balanced draft Fiscal Year 2026-27 Annual Operating Budget and Fiscal Year 2026-31 Five-Year Capital Improvement Plan Budget;  PROVIDE DIRECTION to staff prior to final budget adoption; and RECEIVE input on City Council key initiatives and demands for built environment improvements as well as opportunities to improve organizational efficiency – City Manager’s Office/Finance Department (Shasa Curl/Emily Combs/Mubeen Qader 510-412-2077).
